name: Koulourakia (Sesame Cookies)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F).
2. Cream together butter, caster sugar, and vanilla essence until light and fluffy.
3. Beat eggs in a separate bowl; set aside one tablespoon for glazing. Gradually beat the remaining eggs into the butter mixture, stirring well.
4. Sift plain flour, baking powder, ground cinnamon, and ground cloves twice.
5. Alternately stir the dry ingredients into the creamed mixture with milk to form a soft dough. Knead lightly until smooth. If the dough is sticky, chill for 1 hour.
6. Scatter sesame seeds on one side of a pastry board. Shape the dough into thick pencil shapes and roll them onto the sesame seeds to coat lightly or as desired.
7. Double over the rolled dough and twist, or form into rings, figure eights, or coils.
8. Place the cookies on greased baking sheets. Brush with the reserved egg beaten with a little milk.
9. Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack and store in an airtight container.
Variation: Roll the dough in granulated sugar instead of sesame seeds, and glaze lightly with milk.
